expectations.text: Explains SingleLocationStrategy ships all items from one warehouse
expectations.evidence: Section '1. SingleLocationStrategy (Default)' states Goal: 'Ship the entire order from a single warehouse location' and How it works: 'Finds warehouse locations that have ALL required products' and 'Ships everything from that one location'

expectations.text: Explains MostAbundantStrategy ships from location with highest stock per product
expectations.evidence: Section '2. MostAbundantStrategy' states Goal: 'Ship each product from the warehouse with the highest stock for that product' and How it works: 'For each product, finds the location with maximum available quantity'
Section header reads '1. SingleLocationStrategy (Default)' and configuration example shows 'strategy: SINGLE_LOCATION  # Change to MOST_ABUNDANT' with SINGLE_LOCATION as the current value

expectations.text: Explains how to configure via application.yml
expectations.evidence: Section 'Option 1: Configuration File (Recommended)' shows editing 'Production: /onlineshopapi/src/main/resources/application.yml' with YAML example: 'app:\n  order:\n    strategy: SINGLE_LOCATION  # Change to MOST_ABUNDANT'

expectations.text: Shows the configuration property path: app.order.strategy
expectations.evidence: Configuration examples show 'app:\n  order:\n    strategy:' and environment variable section shows 'export APP_ORDER_STRATEGY=MOST_ABUNDANT' which maps to the app.order.strategy property path

expectations.text: Lists the valid values: SINGLE_LOCATION and MOST_ABUNDANT
expectations.evidence: Section 'Valid Strategy Values' explicitly lists: '- SINGLE_LOCATION - Use SingleLocationStrategy (default)' and '- MOST_ABUNDANT - Use MostAbundantStrategy' with note 'These are defined as an enum in OrderStrategyConfig.java'

expectations.text: Explains the tradeoffs between strategies (simplicity vs optimization)
expectations.evidence: Each strategy lists Advantages/Disadvantages. SingleLocationStrategy Advantages: 'Lower shipping costs (single shipment), Simpler logistics coordination' Disadvantages: 'May fail if no single location has all items, Doesn't optimize for available inventory'. MostAbundantStrategy Advantages: 'Better inventory utilization, More likely to fulfill orders' Disadvantages: 'Higher shipping costs (multiple shipments possible), More complex logistics'. Section 'When to Use Each Strategy' further clarifies tradeoffs.

claims.claim: Strategy can be changed via environment variable (APP_ORDER_STRATEGY)
claims.evidence: Option 2: Environment Variable section shows 'export APP_ORDER_STRATEGY=MOST_ABUNDANT' and inline usage 'APP_ORDER_STRATEGY=MOST_ABUNDANT mvn spring-boot:run'

claims.claim: Strategy can also be changed via JVM system property
claims.evidence: Option 3: JVM System Property section shows 'mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=local -Dapp.order.strategy=MOST_ABUNDANT'

claims.claim: OrderStrategy interface defines one method: findStocks
claims.evidence: The Strategy Interface section shows 'public interface OrderStrategy { List<Stock> findStocks(Set<OrderDetail> orderDetails); }' with comment 'This simple interface defines one method'

claims.claim: Application restart is required after changing strategy
claims.evidence: Important note states 'After changing the strategy: 1. Restart the application' and Runtime Behavior section explains strategy is 'injected at runtime' during application startup

claims.claim: With SINGLE_LOCATION, all products have same shipped_from_id; with MOST_ABUNDANT, products may have different shipped_from_id values
claims.evidence: Example Output section states 'With SINGLE_LOCATION: All products in an order will have the same shipped_from_id' and 'With MOST_ABUNDANT: Products in an order may have different shipped_from_id values (split shipment)'

eval_feedback.overall: Strong, comprehensive assertions that test genuine understanding. The tradeoffs assertion is particularly valuable as it requires substantive explanation beyond surface features. All assertions verify meaningful technical and operational knowledge.
